name: Havoc-C2-SSRF-to-RCE
summary:
Havoc-C2-SSRF-to-RCE
This is a modified version of the CVE-2024-41570 SSRF PoC from [@chebuya](https://github.com/chebuya/Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc) chained with the auth RCE exploit from [@hyperreality](https://github.com/IncludeSecurity/c2-vulnerabilities/tree/main/havoc_auth_rce). This exploit executes code remotely to a target due to multiple vulnerabilities in [Havoc C2 Framework.](https://github.com/HavocFramework/Havoc)

documentation.readme: Havoc-C2-SSRF-to-RCE This is a modified version of the CVE-2024-41570 SSRF PoC from @chebuya chained with the auth RCE exploit from @hyperreality. This exploit executes code remotely to a target due to multiple vulnerabilities in Havoc C2 Framework.

examples: usage: CVE-2024-41570.py [-h] -t TARGET -i IP -p PORT -U USERNAME -P PASSWORD=$KFIOSA_SECRET USER_AGENT] [-H HOSTNAME] [-d DOMAIN_NAME]

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with wiliam227user/CVE-2018-12633-TPLink-Auth-Bypass (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ninjazan420/CVE-2026-1729-PoC-AdForest-WordPress-Authentication-Bypass (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ninjazan420/CVE-2025-52691-PoC-SmarterMail-authentication-bypass-exploit-WT-2026-0001 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_windows', 'shell_linux', 'ad'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use AFTER the chain has gained a shell on the target. Each post-exploit method is per-step ACCEPT gated.
Use as a candidate follow-up for credential harvesting, lateral movement, or persistence — never inlined into argv.
